goal: Verify that the selected public Sentinel-2 L2A COG candidates can be accessed with byte-range reads before committing any full-resolution bands, source-drape replacements, or production masks.

source_context.provider: Element 84 / AWS Open Data Sentinel-2 L2A COG mirror
source_context.catalog_context: Copernicus Data Space Sentinel-2 metadata selected January 19, 2025 as the first low-cloud Pacuare candidate.

allowed_use: future cloud and SCL QA planning
forbidden_use: water, vegetation, canopy, wet-rock, waterfall, mist, or cloud-shadow mask authority
claiming full Pacuare high-resolution imagery coverage
claiming lifelike or photoreal Pacuare imagery evidence

readiness_summary.technical_access_decision: Public COG assets are reachable and support range reads; a small 17PKM TCI review PNG is attached, but the next production step should be a proper raster window reader rather than full-file commits.
readiness_summary.current_tooling_gap: The local validation environment does not have GDAL/rasterio available, so corridor-clipped COG reads and SCL/cloud masking are not yet implemented.

promotion_blockers: Add or vendor an approved raster window reader such as GDAL/rasterio/rio-tiler before deriving corridor clips.
Clip both intersecting MGRS tiles to reviewed Pacuare corridor bounds and stitch in a selected working CRS.
Use SCL/QA plus manual visual review for cloud, shadow, haze, water, canopy, wet rock, waterfall, and mist classification.
Record Sentinel legal notice, attribution, storage/LFS policy, and package redistribution review before committing any full-resolution bands or derivatives.
Compare selected imagery date/season against rainfall, guide/outfitter notes, and flow visual bands before using it for river color, wetness, or gameplay tuning.
next_actions: Add a raster-window extraction dependency or standalone COG reader behind a small fixture test.
Generate tiny corridor-clipped review thumbnails from TCI plus SCL for both Pacuare tiles without committing full COGs.
Only after terms and QA pass, replace the MODIS/GIBS Pacuare source drape with a Sentinel-derived or first-party/permissioned aerial drape.
